entries.reflection: The Great Wall of China, stretching 21,196 kilometers across 7th-century BC to Ming dynasty timelines, was designed to repel invaders. Yet 2,200 years later, the Rosetta Stone became humanity's most celebrated bridge: a 196 BCE granodiorite slab with three scripts that unlocked Egyptian hieroglyphs.
